solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int maxSatisfied(int[] customers, int[] grumpy, int minutes) {
        int n = customers.length;
        int totalSatisfied = 0;
        int maxExtra = 0;
        int extra = 0;

        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            if (grumpy[i] == 0) {
                totalSatisfied += customers[i];
            }
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < minutes; i++) {
            if (grumpy[i] == 1) {
                extra += customers[i];
            }
        }

        maxExtra = extra;

        for (int i = minutes; i < n; i++) {
            if (grumpy[i] == 1) {
                extra += customers[i];
            }
            if (grumpy[i - minutes] == 1) {
                extra -= customers[i - minutes];
            }
            maxExtra = Math.max(maxExtra, extra);
        }

        return totalSatisfied + maxExtra;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution calculates the base number of satisfied customers when the owner is not grumpy. It then uses a sliding window to find the maximum additional customers that can be satisfied by using the secret technique for `minutes` consecutive minutes. This approach ensures optimal performance with linear time complexity and constant space usage.
